memoryArtifacts.headline: The Goodrich family in America. by Goodrich-Family-Memorial Association; Case, Lafayette Wallace, b. 1845, ed pg 34
Founders' Stone for Norwalk, CT
memoryArtifacts.description:
The founding settlers of Norwalk, as listed on the Founders Stone were:

George Abbitt, Robert Beacham, Stephen Beckwith, John Bowton, Matthew Campfield, Nathaniel Eli, Thomas Fitch, John Griggorie, Samuel Hales, Thomas Hales, Walter Haite, Nathaniel Haies, Rev. Thomas Hanford, Richard Homes, Ralph Keiler, Daniel Kellogge, Thomas Lupton, Matthew Marvin, Sr., Matthew Marvin, Jr., Isacke More, Jonathan Marsh, Widow Morgan, Richard Olmsted, Nathaniel Richards, John Ruskoe, Matthias Sention, Sr., Matthias Sention, Jr., Matthew Sention, Thomas Seamer, Richard Webb, and Walter Keiler.

memoryArtifacts.headline: The Adventurer's Boulder:  located in Hartford, CT
memoryArtifacts.description:
The plaque reads:

In Memory of the Courageous 
Adventurers 
Who Inspired and Directed by 
Thomas Hooker Journeyed Though the 
Wilderness from Newton (Cambridge) 
in the Massachusetts Bay to 
Suckiaug (Hartford) - October, 1635

Matthew Allyn      William Lewis 
John Barnard           Mathew Marvin 
William Butler          James Olmsted 
Clement Chaplin      William Pantry 
Nicholas Clarke      Thomas Scott 
Robert Day             Timothy Stanley 
Edward Elmer          Thomas Stanley 
Nathaniel Ely          Edward Stebbins 
Richard Goodman      John Steele 
William Goodwin      John Stone 
Stephen Hart      John Talcott 
William Kelsey      Richard Webb 
William Westwood 

From the Society of the Descendants 
of the Founders of Hartford 
To the People of Hartford 
October 15, 1935
memoryArtifacts.place: Hartford, Colony of Connecticut, British Colonial America

memoryArtifacts.headline: Founders Monument: listing the original founders of Hartford, CT
memoryArtifacts.description:
Founders Monument: 

The original brownstone monument erected in 1837 was replaced by this one in 1986. It stands in the Ancient Burying Ground, which is located to the rear of the First Congregational Church at the corner of Main and Gold Streets in Hartford. This cemetery is also known as Old Center Cemetery. It lists the original Founders of Hartford.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Matthew Marvin於1600年3月26日之前出生在英國艾可格亞特 本特利時，他的父親Edward Marvin51歲，母親Margaret46歲。他於1622年1月與Elizabeth在英國艾可蕾托 本特利聖 馬也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼並於1635年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可被列為judge, wheelwright。他去世於1678年12月26日之後在美國康乃狄克州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可易亞斯特 諾瓦爾可 希斯托利卡爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matthew Marvin于1600年3月26日之前出生在英国艾可格亞特 本特利时，他的父亲Edward Marvin51岁，母亲Margaret46岁。他于1622年1月与Elizabeth在英国艾可蕾托 本特利聖 馬也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他于1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼并于1635年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可被列为judge, wheelwright。他去世于1678年12月26日之后在美国康涅狄格州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德諾瓦爾可易亞斯特 諾瓦爾可 希斯托利卡爾 其特利。

meanings: English (mainly Leicestershire and Lincolnshire): from the Middle English personal name Merefin, Merevin, Old English Merefin. This is probably formed with Old English mere ‘sea’ + Finn (a personal name derived from the ethnic name). Alternatively, it may be a borrowing of an unrecorded Old Norse name Møra-Finnr.
Slovenian: nickname from marvin, the name of a tart light red wine.

variants.name: Murfin
meanings: English (Derbyshire): probably from the Middle English personal name Mer(e)fin; see Marvin .

History: The first Murfins in North America were Nottinghamshire Quakers. Robert and Ann Murfin and their daughter Mary sailed from Hull, England, in 1678 on the ship Shield of Stockton and settled at Chesterfield, near Burlington, NJ.

nameMeanings.name: Matthew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

historicEvents.description: The Dutch discovered the Connecticut Region in 1614, but the European settlement of the region was largely the work of the English Puritans from Massachusetts.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

EARLIEST RECORDED MARKER Nathaniel Hayes BIRTH 1610 England DEATH 1635 (aged 24–25) Norwalk, Fairfield County, Connecticut, USA BURIAL East Norwalk Historical Cemetery Norwalk, Fairfield County, Connecticut, USA MEMORIAL ID 139814054 · View Source

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Matthew Marvin was born before 26 March 1600, in Great Bentley, Essex, England, his father, Edward Marvin, was 51 and his mother, Margaret, was 46. He married Elizabeth in January 1622, in St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 6 daughters. He immigrated to Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1635 and lived in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America in 1635. His occupation is listed as judge, wheelwright in Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. He died after 26 December 1678, in Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut, United States, and was buried in East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matthew Marvin est né avant 26 mars 1600, à Great Bentley, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Edward Marvin, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth en janvier 1622, à St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 6 filles. Il a immigré au Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1635 et a vécu à Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1635. Sa profession de son indiquée est judge, wheelwright à Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé après 26 décembre 1678, à Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut, États-Unis, et a été enterré à East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matthew Marvin vor 26. März 1600 in Great Bentley, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Marvin 51 und seine Mutter Margaret 46. Er hat im Januar 1622 in St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, England Elizabeth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 6 Töchter. Er ist 1635 nach Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. 1635 hat er in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. sein Beruf ist als judge, wheelwright in Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America aufgeführt. Er starb nach 26. Dezember 1678 in Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Marvin è nato il prima del 26 marzo 1600 nel Great Bentley, Essex, England, il suo padre, Edward Marvin, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Margaret, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth nel gennaio 1622, nel St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 6 figlie. Immigrato nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1635 e vissuto a Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America nel 1635. La sua occupazione è indicata come judge, wheelwright nel Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Morì dopo il 26 dicembre 1678, nel Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America, e fu sepolto a East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Matthew Marvinが1600年3月26日以前にイギリスエセックスグレアト ベントリーで生まれた時，父Edward Marvinは51歳，母Margaretは46歳であった。1622年1月にイギリスエセックスリットル ベントリースト マリ'ス チャーチでElizabethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1635年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーに移住し，1635年にはイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードに住んでいた。イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ファイルフィールドノーウォークでの職業はjudge, wheelwrightと記録されている。彼は1678年12月26日以後にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ファイルフィールドノーウォークで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ファイルフィールドノーウォークエアスト ノーウォーク ヒストリカル セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matthew Marvin(이)가 1600년 3월 26일 이전 영국 에세스 그리트 벤틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Marvin(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 46세였다. 그는 1622년 1월 영국 에세스 리틀 벤틀리 스트 마이'스 처르치에서 Elizabeth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1635년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이로 이민을 떠났고, 1635년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 노워크에서 judge, wheelwright이었다. 그는 1678년 12월 26일 이후 미국 코네티컷 주 파어필드 노워크에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 노워크 이스트 노워크 히스토리칼 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Marvin nasceu antes 26 de março de 1600, em Great Bentley, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Marvin, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Margaret, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth em janeiro de 1622, em St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 6 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1635 e viveu em Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America em 1635. Sua profissão aparece como judge, wheelwright em Norwalk, Fairfield, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu após 26 de dezembro de 1678, em Norwalk, Fairfield, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, e foi sepultado em East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matthew Marvin родился перед 26 марта 1600 г. в местности грит бентли, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Marvin было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth январь 1622 г. в местности ст мэри'с чурч, литл бентли, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 6 дочерей. Он иммигрировал в массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1635 году, а также жил в местности хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1635 году.  Он работал по профессии judge, wheelwright в местности норвалк, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер после 26 декабрь 1678 г. в местности норвалк, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты и был похоронен в местности эаст норвалк хисторикаль кеметири, норвалк, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matthew Marvin nació antes de 26 de marzo de 1600, en Great Bentley, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Marvin, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Margaret, tenía 46 años. Se casó con Elizabeth en enero de 1622, en St Mary's Church, Little Bentley, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 6 hijas. Inmigró a Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1635 y vivió en Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America en 1635. Su ocupación registrada era judge, wheelwright en Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Murió después de 26 de diciembre de 1678, en Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, y fue sepultado en East Norwalk Historical Cemetery, Norwalk, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
